blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: So Cade was alone on the beach. I was alone upstairs. Dean was alone in the kitchen. Wren was mostly alone at the water.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: And Marcus was upstairs. Alone. Door locked.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: So we were all just — separately, all at the same time, with no one to confirm where we were.
She wasn't accusing anyone. She was doing the same math Dean had started. Just arriving at the answer out loud, for the whole room to hear.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: That's not unusual. We were on a beach trip. People drift apart. Nobody signs in and out.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: I know. I'm just saying how it looks.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: It looks like a normal afternoon.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: It looks like nobody can confirm where anyone was for close to an hour.
Cade pushed back from the table slightly. Not leaving — just needing space between himself and what was being said.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: We're talking about ourselves like we're— like any of us would actually—

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: These are us. We've known each other since college.
The ocean came through the screen. A cloud crossed the sun and the kitchen dimmed for a moment and then brightened again.
